mission.title: thanos: query: with grafana gives inconsistent dashboard with every refresh
mission.description: query: with grafana gives inconsistent dashboard with every refresh. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify thanos troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your thanos deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos -n thanos --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review thanos configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant thanos configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
kubectl get configmap -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=thanos
```
**Thanos, Prometheus and Golang version used**:
* Thanos: 11.1.0, deployed through [Bitnami helm chart](https://github.com/bitnami/charts/tree/master/bitnami/thanos), quay.io/thanos/thanos:v0.25.2
* Prometheus: 0.57.0 deployed through

steps.title: Apply the fix for query: with grafana gives inconsistent dashboard with every…
steps.description:
Fix a data race between Respond() and query/queryRange functions by
returning an extra optional function from instrumented functions that
releases the resources i.e. calls Close().

Cannot reproduce the following race:

```
==================
WARNING: DATA RACE
Write at 0x00c00566fa00 by goroutine
```yaml
thanos:
  storegateway:
    enabled: false
  query:
    enabled: true
    replicaLabel:
      - prometheus_replica
    dnsDiscovery:
      sidecarsService: 'prometheus-stack-kube-prom-thanos-discovery'
      sidecarsNamespace: 'prometheus'

kube-prometheus-stack:
  grafana:
    sidecar:
      datasources:
        url: http://prometheus-stack-thanos-query:9090/
        initDatasources: true
      dashboards:
        searchNamespace: ALL
        labelValue: null # Needs to be null in order to load our dashboards
  prometheus:
    replicas: 3
    thanosService:
      enabled: true
    thanosServi
```

steps.title: Confirm query: with grafana gives inconsistent dashboard… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos -n thanos --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n thanos --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Fix a data race between Respond() and query/queryRange functions by
returning an extra optional function from instrumented functions that
releases the resources i.e. calls Close().
codeSnippets:
thanos:
  storegateway:
    enabled: false
  query:
    enabled: true
    replicaLabel:
      - prometheus_replica
    dnsDiscovery:
      sidecarsService: 'prometheus-stack-kube-prom-thanos-discovery'
      sidecarsNamespace: 'prometheus'

kube-prometheus-stack:
  grafana:
    sidecar:
      datasources:
        url: http://prometheus-stack-thanos-query:9090/
        initDatasources: true
      dashboards:
        searchNamespace: ALL
        labelValue: null # Needs to be null in order to load our dashboards
  prometheus:
    replicas: 3
    thanosService:
      enabled: true
    thanosServiceMonitor:
      enabled: true
    service:
      sessionAffinity: 'ClientIP'
    prometheusSpec:
      thanos:
        objectStorageConfig:
          key: config-file.yaml
          name: thanos-secr

- name: kube-prometheus-stack
    version: 36.6.2
    repository: https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
  - name: thanos
    version: 10.5.5
    repository: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with Thanos deployed (sidecar, query, store gateway, compactor as needed). The thanos binary provides subcommands for each component.
